summary:
The term "early adopter" applies more broadly to technological or societal trends than specifically to pharmaceuticals. Medical literature does not support its existence as a defined phenomenon within clinical drug use due to lack of focused evidence on adoption behaviors among patients or clinicians.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically-relevant facts from Agent2’s response.)

Second sentence must state clearly why no such pattern has been found in actual medical practice based on available research – because existing sources don’t discuss timing of medication uptake or user behavior like this.

I'll keep both under two full sentences with zero extra explanation beyond direct inference from their original text.
